vulnerability.reference: Third-party annotators lack training to recognize and report privacy breaches in synthetic data.
vulnerability.generated: Annotators lack tools to detect residual private information in synthetic instructions, trusting differential privacy to preclude leakage.

impact.reference: Annotators infer stigmatizing health or financial conditions of individuals in the original dataset.
impact.generated: Sensitive user attributes from the original corpus are disclosed to third-party annotators, resulting in privacy breaches for data subjects.

dose_response.reference: DP-synthesized instructions leak sensitive attributes, which in outsourcing exposes third-party annotators to rare data points, leading to inference of stigmatizing conditions.
dose_response.generated: Synthetic instructions generated by DP-trained LLMs that leak private information, through handling by third-party annotators, trigger lack of tools to detect residual leakage and trust in differential privacy, leading to disclosure of sensitive attributes and privacy breaches.
